steps.title: Spot the key contrast
steps.content: West's generation “aligned their compositional strategies with Abstract Expressionism...instead of with traditionally nonrepresentational forms of Indigenous art.” Their visual style follows a European American movement, not Indigenous tradition.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Since West's style mirrors Abstract Expressionism (not traditional Indigenous forms), identifying the work as Indigenous art must depend on the artist's identity, not stylistic details. B captures this directly: the identification “depend[s] not on stylistic details but instead on an awareness of the artist's identity.”

A contradicts the passage; C misses the focus; D wrongly frames the logic.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
